steps.title: Identify the logical impossibility that reveals false performance
steps.content: Richard explicitly says he imprisoned Clarence (“I indeed have cast in darkness”) but then reveals he weeps to gullible people about it (“I do beweep”). This logical impossibility—openly admitting to causing something while publicly mourning it—signals that his display of grief is a false performance.

steps.title: Confirm the three-part deception structure
steps.content: D identifies the full deception: Richard's false performance of grief, his false blame placed on the Queen, and his actual responsibility for Clarence's troubles. The other three choices fail to capture this complete picture. That matches choice D — D is correct.
